reviews.review:
Gameplay Mechanics - 9/10: Showcases possibilities in many curious ways, but wish there were more to explore.
Graphics & Visuals - 9/10: Depicts real world items pretty well.
Narrative - 2/10: I mean we could get a lot more type of inner games to explore possibilities, not those lame demos.
Sound & Music - 9/10: Simple and suits well.
Replayability - 3/10: Could be explored only once, you could try to beat your own records in lame games.
Innovation - 7/10: Actually the idea behind all of this is quite cool, the realization is poor in the end.
Technical Performance - 10/10: Perfect.
Art Direction/Design - 9/10: Cute and smooth art style, very appealing and seamless.
Monetization/Value - 0/10: That's a dumb decision to sell it...

Total - 6/10

This is a disgrace to Nintendo. It forces you to collect all the stamps before you can move on to the next scene, you can't freely access the instruction manual, there are outdated and idiotic quizzes, uninteresting mini-games, and ridiculous scene displays. As a Nintendo fan, I felt humiliated after playing it. As many have said, it should have been free, but Nintendo's arrogance in this generation prevented them from doing so, instead making users pay to experience a pile of crap. Making such a product makes me furious. I don't recommend buying it; it will only waste your precious time.

It's decent enough to learn more about the console, but it should be free. The demos and minigames could be better, the latter ones overuse mouse mode. Plus, it forces you to buy extra accessories for some of these experiences. The biggest problem with this "game" is that Astro's Playroom already exists and came free with the console.
I actually enjoyed learning about how the switch was made. Some of the mini games are pretty fun too
Are you serious ? Charging ANYTHING for this is absolutely baffling. Nintendo is the worst company in the world
A complete Joke to charge money for this. Bland, uninspired, boring and a reminder that Nintendo became a soulless Company that doesn't even try to hide the fact.
this game is like scrambled eggs it looks like gosh darn piss and it is gosh darn piss
this is a borring/basic tutorial and wtf 10 dollars!?!?!?!??!?!!?!?!?!?!?!?
ITS way to hard for Kids. My Kid cried because this Game was way to hard for him and even i didn't make it

I haven’t finished the game because the game literally doesn’t let me. 

This game is supposed to show the user everything about the Switch 2, its gimmicks, specialties and more. A tech demo you have to pay 10 euros for. I honestly didn’t really mind spending a few bucks on a game that, in my opinion, should be free, but now I fully regret it. You can play the game for quite a while until you get to specific mini-games that require your monitor or TV to meet certain requirements, or that you need a specific accessory for. This is outrageous!So now you’ve paid for a game you can’t even finish because you need extra requirements on top of it! NOWHERE on the Nintendo eShop does it say that this is necessary (I'm talking about Monitor/TV requierments, it does say you might need certain accessories). So what looks like a 10-euro game stacks up to a 500+ euro game if you don’t already have all the accessories and equipment requirements! Absolutely outrageous! This game should have come free with the Switch 2!

Who decided this undercooked instruction manual is worth $10 + all the console accessories, if you want the whole experience of this literal tech demo. Wii Sports, Nintendo Land, and Bomberman Act Zero on the Xbox 360. outshine this game in every single category.
10 dollars for a tech demo, 10 dollars for a tech demo when deltarunen chapters / and 2 are free

They're charging money for the instructions manual of a console that already had a big price bump.
And it's not even a good experience, it's just tedium, tests and a couple of 'barely' minigames. What a joke of a product.
Should have been free. Even then, it still wouldn't be worth it since you can't 100% the game without purchasing other accessories that hasn't even come out yet.
As a pack in game I would have added a point or two as it is it’s a serviceable manual without the usual Nintendo charm.

€10 for a Wiki page, no thanks
And you need ALL THE ACCESSORIES (even the joy con wheel) to complete the game...

it is a nice game for those who are interessted in technical specifications and want to learn more about there console, all in all a good game, i had my fun with this game, should be free nethertheless
Definitely one of the more lamer games Nintendo have made...beat it in less than 2 hours. Two thumbs down.
If theres any game that does not deserve the hate it gets its this gem this is a relaxing experience with super fun activities.

An incisive academic journey that’s absolutely worth the price of admission. If you think it should’ve been free, you clearly don’t grasp what it’s for. Want a free manual? Check the box — it won’t tell you why anything was done the way it was, and it definitely won’t walk you through it interactively. "But hey, it’s free"
The switch 2 welcome tour is a meh tech demo That makes it even more meh because of its price. Astro bot is free and is better than this worthless scam.

Man I really wanted to like this. Outside of the very very very obvious flaw that it should have been included it’s just kinda mostly meh. 

The good: design ideas are fun of exploring the device, nice clean design. It’s kinda fun finding all the components.

Bad: mini games are ridiculously uninspired, characters around are super generic, you find random generic items (why not find like a game boy or something fun instead of like a shoe). The quizzes are just kinda time consuming,

The entire thing feels like some beta idea that should have been fleshed outs. How are 90% of these demos not like an alpha build thing, they’re devoid of any character or care. Someone slapped them together in an afternoon it looks **** not do them like Mario party style??

Man what a disappointment
Charge money for this game is a straight disrespect to all of Nintendo's fans and customers

Charging me for a welcome tour is not very welcoming. I think this is the last the true showcase of how far Nintendo's greed has gone, and how it will never end, so long as we support them.

They clearly no longer care about user experience and just want to exploit us for every penny they can get.
Overpriced  (Placeholder Text) (Placeholder Text) (Placeholder Text) (Placeholder Text)

An infinite sea of trash on a trash platform made of trashy trash from my trash bin.  Seriously, this is insulting to have to pay for, and Nintendo needs to get its head out of its butt if it thinks this is worth paying for.
The game itself is an 8/10, it's fun, it's quiet difficult to 100%, it has a lot of content, but the price, even if it's low, it could be cool to play for free when purchasing a Switch 2

I am mad at myself for buying this. Nintendo charged so much more than anything previously for its new console, and then decided that for their promotional tech demo game to show off the features and functions of it, they would charge another $10. It's got to be one of the worst, most greedy looks for a game company in a long time. 

That said, I am absolutely the type of person who loves to really take the time to explore and learn about all of the unique and cool new aspects of a piece of tech that I bought, and Welcome Tour really gives a thorough walkthrough of everything about the system in a very palatable way. I enjoyed learning about some of the thoughtful physical design aspects of the controllers and intentional placements of inner components of the system's components just as much as I liked the tech demos that showed off the new or improved capabilities of the system. It gives you a chance to experience what the system has to offer visually, audibly, or physically, through cool little demonstrations. 

The actual minigames, which are likely the only part that would merit charging money for this game, might have been my least favorite part, as most of them require you to constantly shuffle between control styles, docked/handheld mode, or to use various accessories. You cannot actually complete all of the challenges without owning the appropriate accessories in some cases, either - despite them even providing a way to "skip" those challenges... but this does not actually give you progress towards completion.

In all, I did enjoy strolling around the game system's surfaces, learning some cool little insights into the development of the system, and I did leave with an added respect and appreciation for how many decisions and careful considerations had to be weighed in creating this next-gen hybrid console. However, the frustrations with minigame progression and requirements to own extra accessories to finish it are small marks against the game as a whole. The horrifically greedy and tasteless decision to charge $10 for this tech demo is what brings the marks down to a 5 here. 

Final recommendation: If you absolutely love learning about your new tech, and want to do so in a creative and somewhat entertaining way, go for it. Otherwise, please do not support this type of corporate behavior and skip this.

Disappointingly Underwhelming – Nintendo Switch 2 Welcome Tour Misses the MarkI had high hopes going into the Nintendo Switch 2 Welcome Tour, especially with all the hype around the next generation of hybrid gaming. Unfortunately, the event felt more like a missed opportunity than a true celebration of innovation.First off, the demo stations were painfully limited. For a console that’s supposedly launching with a powerful new chipset and an OLED Pro display, you’d think Nintendo would let fans spend more time with actual gameplay. Instead, we were stuck watching curated trailers or playing brief, scripted demos that didn’t showcase much more than what’s already available online. Where was the promised leap in performance?Second, the titles available were either ports or sequels that looked nearly indistinguishable from what we’ve already played on the original Switch. Sure, Mario Kart X and Zelda: Echoes of the Past looked polished, but they didn't scream “next-gen” the way you'd expect. And shockingly, there was no mention of backward compatibility—a baffling omission that left a lot of returning fans confused and **** staff, while enthusiastic, didn’t seem prepared to answer deeper questions about the hardware, cloud features, or digital game transfer. The few tech details we got felt carefully managed, almost as if Nintendo was holding back instead of building excitement.Lastly, the merchandise and fan engagement felt shallow—lots of QR codes and photo ops, but little in the way of meaningful interaction. For an event that’s meant to welcome both new and longtime fans, it felt more like a glorified commercial than an immersive experience.Nintendo has the magic, but this tour didn’t capture it. Here’s hoping the actual console release will do what this event couldn’t: convince us the Switch 2 is more than just an incremental upgrade.
Great game to explore Nintendo Switch 2! I like to see how is going on switch 2. It is kinds fun when you play with open mind.

DO NOT BUY THIS. I bought this and while I don't believe I was "robbed" this is really not worth anything above like 1 dollar, it is full of shallow tech-demos, the ONLY reason you would find any value in this game is if you are really interested in technical stuff, and even then it doesn't consistantly deliver even that.
To get more into my experience, there were some tech demos I liked, more specifically the upscaling tech demo (which I found was the best tech demo I have seen for upscalers), some facts I liked about how the console was engineered because I'm into engineering myself, but I must be clear, THIS IS NOT A GAME, if you are looking for a game just look elsewhere, if you are starved for a 120 fps experience on switch 2 this doesn't deliver anything at all, and pretty much ANYTHING else please just watch a video on youtube.

Genuinely had a lot of fun with this. Enjoyed learning about the console and the mini games were fun. I'm definitely in the minority and was sceptical before purchasing, but I really had fun with this game.
This game is only a small introduction with tutorials or text. It's like here is switch 2 that can do this things and most is text only some small Minigames.

While this “Game” (Tech demo) does present some interesting insights into how certain parts of the controller were designed (I.E using magnets and using the Sr and Sl buttons as steel to attach to the side of the console this should be FREE information on a FREE tech demo.£8 (Or any price for that matter) is a absolute travesty and turns what could of been a fun tech demo into a overpriced piece of garbage.

For comparison, for that same price, I could buy Undertale on steam for that price which is a full game with multiple endings, interesting character and deep lore. Game is “affordable” because AAA companies have gotten gamers used to paying £60 standard for a new game but take a look at indies and you shall see the value is inflated and experiences like this aren’t worth your time.
The biggest childlike overreaction in gaming I've seen yet. Far better put together and more enjoyable than you're being told.

This is quite a nothing burger. Charging any amount of money for this shows yet again, Nintendo's refusal to understand where their product actually sits in quality. The mini-games were mildly interesting but I have no interest in going back to any of them and since I don't have every accessory, I can't even finish some of the mini games. If this was packed in for free with the console, I'd say this is interesting, albeit lazy. Since it's a product with its own price tag, I have to say it's an embarrassment.

Welcome Tour isn’t really a game, it’s really the equivalent of bonus features on a Blu-ray disguised as a game. Some people love that, some don’t : and that’s precisely why I would’ve made a terrible pack-in title ! 

This is bonus content for people who want bonus content. If it was a pack-in people would’ve complained about it anyway and asked Nintendo to remove it from the console to lower the price.

However, if you like behind-the-scenes type of content and/or loved science museums when you were a kid... this is pure gold. I was genuinely fascinated by all the "design secrets" Nintendo was willing to share. I would often pick up my console or dock to marvel at a tiny detail made to make our life easier after seeing it in one of the information panel.


The game is beautiful and fun, the dialogues are charming and I really didn’t mind the "stamp collecting" thing. I wasn’t blown away by the technical demos and mini-games, but they were fun (some were terribly addictive and challenging, though !). It shows what could be possible in the future and I hope devs will actually use the console’s features more than Switch 1’s game.

All in all, the drama surrounding it was incredibly stupid because that’s truly a beautifully crafted experience that’s well worth its price. Get it if you find the concept appealing. Ignore it, if you think it’s not for you.

The game itself is an 8/10, it's fun, it's quiet difficult to 100%, it has a lot of content, but the price, even if it's low, it could be cool to play for free when purchasing a Switch 2

I am mad at myself for buying this. Nintendo charged so much more than anything previously for its new console, and then decided that for their promotional tech demo game to show off the features and functions of it, they would charge another $10. It's got to be one of the worst, most greedy looks for a game company in a long time. 

That said, I am absolutely the type of person who loves to really take the time to explore and learn about all of the unique and cool new aspects of a piece of tech that I bought, and Welcome Tour really gives a thorough walkthrough of everything about the system in a very palatable way. I enjoyed learning about some of the thoughtful physical design aspects of the controllers and intentional placements of inner components of the system's components just as much as I liked the tech demos that showed off the new or improved capabilities of the system. It gives you a chance to experience what the system has to offer visually, audibly, or physically, through cool little demonstrations. 

The actual minigames, which are likely the only part that would merit charging money for this game, might have been my least favorite part, as most of them require you to constantly shuffle between control styles, docked/handheld mode, or to use various accessories. You cannot actually complete all of the challenges without owning the appropriate accessories in some cases, either - despite them even providing a way to "skip" those challenges... but this does not actually give you progress towards completion.

In all, I did enjoy strolling around the game system's surfaces, learning some cool little insights into the development of the system, and I did leave with an added respect and appreciation for how many decisions and careful considerations had to be weighed in creating this next-gen hybrid console. However, the frustrations with minigame progression and requirements to own extra accessories to finish it are small marks against the game as a whole. The horrifically greedy and tasteless decision to charge $10 for this tech demo is what brings the marks down to a 5 here. 

Final recommendation: If you absolutely love learning about your new tech, and want to do so in a creative and somewhat entertaining way, go for it. Otherwise, please do not support this type of corporate behavior and skip this.
Great game to explore Nintendo Switch 2! I like to see how is going on switch 2. It is kinds fun when you play with open mind.

Genuinely had a lot of fun with this. Enjoyed learning about the console and the mini games were fun. I'm definitely in the minority and was sceptical before purchasing, but I really had fun with this game.
The biggest childlike overreaction in gaming I've seen yet. Far better put together and more enjoyable than you're being told.

Welcome Tour isn’t really a game, it’s really the equivalent of bonus features on a Blu-ray disguised as a game. Some people love that, some don’t : and that’s precisely why I would’ve made a terrible pack-in title ! 

This is bonus content for people who want bonus content. If it was a pack-in people would’ve complained about it anyway and asked Nintendo to remove it from the console to lower the price.

However, if you like behind-the-scenes type of content and/or loved science museums when you were a kid... this is pure gold. I was genuinely fascinated by all the "design secrets" Nintendo was willing to share. I would often pick up my console or dock to marvel at a tiny detail made to make our life easier after seeing it in one of the information panel.


The game is beautiful and fun, the dialogues are charming and I really didn’t mind the "stamp collecting" thing. I wasn’t blown away by the technical demos and mini-games, but they were fun (some were terribly addictive and challenging, though !). It shows what could be possible in the future and I hope devs will actually use the console’s features more than Switch 1’s game.

All in all, the drama surrounding it was incredibly stupid because that’s truly a beautifully crafted experience that’s well worth its price. Get it if you find the concept appealing. Ignore it, if you think it’s not for you.
A great introduction demo to the Switch 2, but it would be perfect if it was free. I liked it but I wouldn't buy a thing like this again.

Do not listen to all the horrible reviews. Most of these people never even played the game. For $10 you get a fun and interactive game that tells you all about the Nintendo switch 2, all its features and how it was made. Is it game of the year worthy? No lol. Is it worth $10 and worth playing? Yes. The minigames are fun and use the joy cons to the full extent. In my opinion, this game is worth playing and will give you a few solid hours of gameplay.
This it’s not even a game it’s a cheap demo poorly made. Nothing compares to AstrobotPS5, mini games are basic totally stupid. It will be OK if it’s free but it’s not.

As an engineer, I thoroughly enjoyed this tour of the Nintendo Switch 2. It allowed me to see the new gimmicks of this hardware and the improvements over the Switch 1. The demo was fine and impressive, but it lacked an online scoreboard and some replayability. Overall, this tour served its purpose.

$10? For this masterpiece? That’s not a price — that’s charity. ‘Welcome Tour’ isn’t a game, it’s an experience. It taught me more about my Switch 2 than four years of tech YouTubers ever could. The rumble feedback? Transcendental. The design breakdowns? Oscar-worthy. I walked in expecting a tutorial — I left spiritually fulfilled. Nintendo didn’t have to go this hard… but they did. GOTY 2025, and somehow the best bargain of the decade. If you skip this, you’re skipping the soul of the system

Great game to play to introduce you to the Switch 2. The amount of detail and information you get out of this game is truly unparalleled. This game is honestly a steal at only $10. There's hours and hours of fun and information. One of the best games on the system and easily one of the best games of the year!
game of the years 2025                                                                  ;

I brought it, only negative it was not included in the system for free.  To beat the game you have buy all nessceary accessories.  I see the visions what nintendo trying to do but greedy Corp  management mess that up.

GAME OF THE YEAR! - Just kidding. But this is a relaxing, fun, and interesting piece of software for the Nintendo Switch 2.

The game itself is structured like a real-life museum of the Switch 2, which is unique and cute. The stamp rally, while quite obvious sometimes, gets interesting the longer you play. This is also the same case with the quizzes and insights. There are technical details that go very deep and are quite rare to see in a Nintendo game. This extends to the minigames, which show off mouse controls, GL/GR buttons, gyro controls, and my favorite - a game of Twister where you need to put 10 fingers on the touchscreen at once. The tech demos are quite interesting too, showing DLSS/Super Resolution, HD Rumble 2 -which is so much better than the original, and more. One of my favorites was having to find the singular pixel on the Switch 2's 1080p screen. It has a nice presentation and is worth the 10 bucks. Obviously it should have been a free pack-in title, but for those interested in Nintendo's quirkier romps, this is sure to be a good time.
I don’t understand why this game gets so much bad reviews. It’s 10$ should be free I know. But for what it is a guide it is pretty good. It’s fun to play and the minigames are great.

Its job is to tell people about how the switch 2 works and what it can do, and it does a good job at it. The mini games are fun, and the tech demos let you know how good the Switch 2 is. And yes, it was interesting to find out how the switch 2 does everything. It does a good job as a tech demo. But as an actual game it could do a better job. You get all the stamps and all the game says is nice job. There's no credits, no post game content, no nothing. You get nothing for beating or even 100% beating this game. This game is clearly for a specific group of people and for them it's a great game. I am not in that group of people. I've been defending this game since day one and wanted to give it a good score but that lack of an ending makes it hard. It's still a good game with a fair price and for a tech demo it's pretty solid. But as a game it's a little lacking.

It’s no Astro’s Playroom, but if you want an actual demonstration of all the parts of the Switch, it’s actually better in that respect than Astro’s Playroom was for the PS5. (And really what’s the point of making a free game to show off all the features of the new gamepad, when most of the actual PS5 games barely make any use of them?)
Greatest video game of all time. A true masterpiece, one that will never be replicated in its beauty- gameplay so innovative i could cry and graphics so irredescant i want to dive into my switch 2

Most people giving reviews haven't even played the game, they're just mad that it costs money. 

This is not a user manual, it's an interactive developer commentary + tech demo. It's awesome for what it is and easily worth the $10 if you like learning how stuff works.

At first I thought it was unnecessary to buy a manual for €10 but in the end I bought it and it was fun. The mini-games are fun and enjoyable, there's a lot to discover and I actually found out new information about my console, but it should be free.

ehhhhh it’s okay. I had my fun with it, I don’t care that it was 10 dollars, sure, it should’ve been a pack-in, but whatever. The mini games were decently fun, but getting all the stamps were tedious, the quizzes super obvious, and some pretty cool visuals. it’s not a “instruction manual”, more like if Wii Play had fun facts about the Wii. tech demos were pretty cool showcases as well.
It's just great , loved it ,and while it is nice that it whould be free, it is worth 10 dollars

It's a cool game that teaches you about the console with some fun mini games it isnt revolutionary or anything like that. But a lot of people will still get some fun out of it and its worth a try even though it should of been a pack in

lt's not sounds good for me(before), but when I turn it on, it was good game to learn switch2's features.
but this game must be free!
Must buy, best game of all time, inspiring, destroys Red Dead Redemption 2 and The Mid of Us

Honestly I'm having more fun with this than with Mario Kart. 
Yes it costs money, but it's still nearly 10x cheaper than Mario Kart lol...
I like it,it has cool mini games and you learn about Nintendo Switch 2 there is no bug or any other issues

It's honestly better than expected, some of the little tech demos are pretty cool, the mouse minigames are fun and if you want a cheap gateway into a game that actually uses the Switch 2's mouse capability, here it is. And hey, you might even learn something.

Of course it should have been free, but at the same time, I've bought games that are worse value for money (such as 1-2 Switch, which this is leagues above).
I think it is an amazing game to be honest. It is well made and I learned a lot.

It's $10 guys. We never thought Switch 2 would have a pack in, so let's quit complaining about what Welcome Tour should've been. The game is fun for what it is and for only $10 it's great. It is more niche however, you have to actually be interested in secrets about your console. But to be clear, this is NOT an instruction manual. There are actual minigames and tech demos and you do not need this game to play or understand how to use your console. People rating the game a 0 did not give it any chance and are just raging because they apparently just found out that things cost money. I only rate this an 8 because it is more niche and won't interest everybody

The game/manual is quite fun to be honest, and you can get lost in it for many hours. I agree this game should have been free, but hey, €9,99 isn’t much especially when there’s quite a lot to doin this game!

Gameplay Mechanics - 9/10: Showcases possibilities in many curious ways, but wish there were more to explore.
Graphics & Visuals - 9/10: Depicts real world items pretty well.
Narrative - 2/10: I mean we could get a lot more type of inner games to explore possibilities, not those lame demos.
Sound & Music - 9/10: Simple and suits well.
Replayability - 3/10: Could be explored only once, you could try to beat your own records in lame games.
Innovation - 7/10: Actually the idea behind all of this is quite cool, the realization is poor in the end.
Technical Performance - 10/10: Perfect.
Art Direction/Design - 9/10: Cute and smooth art style, very appealing and seamless.
Monetization/Value - 0/10: That's a dumb decision to sell it...

Total - 6/10

It's decent enough to learn more about the console, but it should be free. The demos and minigames could be better, the latter ones overuse mouse mode. Plus, it forces you to buy extra accessories for some of these experiences. The biggest problem with this "game" is that Astro's Playroom already exists and came free with the console.
I actually enjoyed learning about how the switch was made. Some of the mini games are pretty fun too
As a pack in game I would have added a point or two as it is it’s a serviceable manual without the usual Nintendo charm.

it is a nice game for those who are interessted in technical specifications and want to learn more about there console, all in all a good game, i had my fun with this game, should be free nethertheless
If theres any game that does not deserve the hate it gets its this gem this is a relaxing experience with super fun activities.

An incisive academic journey that’s absolutely worth the price of admission. If you think it should’ve been free, you clearly don’t grasp what it’s for. Want a free manual? Check the box — it won’t tell you why anything was done the way it was, and it definitely won’t walk you through it interactively. "But hey, it’s free"
The switch 2 welcome tour is a meh tech demo That makes it even more meh because of its price. Astro bot is free and is better than this worthless scam.

Man I really wanted to like this. Outside of the very very very obvious flaw that it should have been included it’s just kinda mostly meh.

The entire thing feels like some beta idea that should have been fleshed outs. How are 90% of these demos not like an alpha build thing, they’re devoid of any character or care. Someone slapped them together in an afternoon it looks **** not do them like Mario party style??
